name: Sparkline
goal: Render a small inline trend chart (line, area, or bar) for a numeric series.
description: Compact inline SVG chart for trend visualisation in a row, table cell, or KPI tile. Supports line (default), filled area, and bar shapes; auto-scales to the value range. Values can be supplied four ways (resolution order): (1) JSON-encoded `values` attribute, (2) JS property, (3) a <ce-data> child carrying a {values:[…]} or bare-array JSON payload (CDR-5 advanced shape — useful when the payload is too big or escaping-heavy for an attribute), (4) whitespace- or comma-separated numbers in the element's own text content. The earliest source with data wins. Child-content streaming is supported via MutationObserver, so values appended after upgrade re-render.

props.name: values
props.description: Numeric series. Can be supplied via JSON attribute, JS property, or as whitespace/comma-separated numbers in the element's text content (text-content fallback). Required only when no text-content values are provided.

slots.description: Data slot. Accepts either (a) whitespace- or comma-separated numbers as plain text content, or (b) a <ce-data> child carrying a {values:[…]} or bare-array JSON payload (preferred for large or escaping-heavy data). The slot content is not displayed visually — it acts purely as a data carrier (CDR-2/CDR-5).

sideEffects.description: Installs a MutationObserver on its own subtree to watch for text-content changes when the values attribute is omitted.
sideEffects.reason: Supports streaming data appended to the element after upgrade (CDR-2/CDR-5 text-content fallback). Disconnected on disconnectedCallback.

interchangeableWith.when: shape="bar" and a labelled, full-size bar chart is acceptable

a11y.notes: The SVG carries role=img and an aria-label that summarises the value range and count.
